title: French Revolution vs American Revolution
shortAnswer: American Revolution was conservative colonial revolt; French Revolution was radical internal upheaval against monarchy with much greater violence and social transformation.
prosA: Achieved independence with relatively low violence and bloodshed
Created stable constitutional republic that endured
Preserved beneficial social institutions while gaining freedom
Established precedent for peaceful democratic governance
consA: Failed to address slavery and deep social inequalities
Limited scope of social transformation and reform
prosB: Completely dismantled oppressive feudal social hierarchy
Addressed severe economic inequality and poverty
Spread democratic ideals across Europe rapidly
consB: Extreme violence and mass executions destabilized society
Political instability led to Napoleon's eventual dictatorship
Economic chaos and social upheaval caused widespread suffering
verdict: The American Revolution succeeded through conservative, measured change while the French Revolution achieved broader social transformation through radical means. Both addressed government debt and inequality but with vastly different approaches and outcomes.
